key_insight: The paper presents a fully open, multilingual pipeline that turns unstructured news text into signed, temporal political network graphs by jointly extracting entities and relations at scale.
engineering_relevance: It shows how guided decoding, ontology constraints, and entity linking can make large-scale information extraction more robust and reproducible for computational social science workflows.
method: Span-based NER identifies person and organization mentions in multiple languages.
A three-stage linking cascade resolves mentions to language-independent Wikidata IDs.
A high-throughput mixture-of-experts extractor produces directed, signed relations under a domain ontology.
evidence_signal: Against a 3,491-relation gold standard, the pipeline achieved 68.2% strict and 93.7% lenient textual correctness, and case studies in Austria and Poland matched known political events and networks.
watch_item: Performance depends on ontology quality and entity resolution accuracy, so errors in linking or relation constraints can propagate into the final knowledge graph.
paper_title: Mapping Political-Elite Networks in Europe with a Multilingual Joint Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
